prompt: Cart total is not updating when item quantities change

turns.content:
<task>
The shopping cart total doesn't update when users change item quantities. They have to refresh the page to see the correct total.
</task>

arguments.reasoning:
Two bugs in updateQuantity:
1. Line 31 mutates the item directly instead of creating a new object, so Zustand doesn't detect the change (shallow comparison sees same reference)
2. Line 32 doesn't recalculate the total — it sets items without updating total
Also, the items array itself isn't cloned (line 28 gets reference, line 32 sets same reference).

arguments.message:
Found two bugs in `updateQuantity`:

1. **Mutation without new reference** (line 31): `item.quantity = quantity` mutates the existing object. Zustand uses shallow comparison and won't detect the change since the array reference is the same.
2. **Total not recalculated** (line 32): `set({ items })` doesn't update `total`, so even if the UI re-renders, the total stays stale.

The fix is to create new array/object references and recalculate the total.

arguments.title: Zustand state mutation bug pattern
arguments.content: Never mutate state objects directly in Zustand. Use set() callback with new object references. Common mistake: getting array ref with get(), mutating item, then set({items}) — Zustand won't detect the change. Always use map/filter/spread to create new references.

turns.content: Finding recorded: Zustand state mutation bug pattern

arguments.summary: Fixed cart total bug: updateQuantity was mutating state directly and not recalculating total. Now uses map() for immutable update and recalculates total in the same set() call.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed two bugs in updateQuantity: 1) State was mutated directly instead of creating new references — Zustand couldn't detect the change. 2) Total wasn't being recalculated. Now uses immutable update with map() and recalculates total in the same set() call.
